perturbation.original_text: 5. Term and Termination. This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and will continue until termination as provided below. Either Consultant or Company may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party. Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except: Company shall be obliged to pay, within thirty (30) days after receipt of Consultant's final statement, all amounts owing to Consultant for unpaid Services completed by Consultant and related expenses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 hereof, and Sections 2, 3, S(c), 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term and Termination. This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and will continue until termination as provided below. Consultant may terminate this Agreement only with a mutual written agreement with the Company. The company can terminate the agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party. Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except: Company shall be obliged to pay, within ninety (90) days after receipt of Consultant's final statement, all amounts owing to Consultant for unpaid Services completed by Consultant and related expenses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 hereof, and Sections 2, 3, S(c), 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states either party can terminate with written notice, but the modification states that Consultant can ONLY terminate with MUTUAL WRITTEN AGREEMENT, while the company only needs to provide prior written notice. This creates a contradiction in the terms of termination and when the consultant final payments will be made as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Term and Termination. This Agreement will commence on the Effective Date and will continue until termination as provided below. Consultant may terminate this Agreement only with a mutual written agreement with the Company. The company can terminate the agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party. Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except: Company shall be obliged to pay, within ninety (90) days after receipt of Consultant's final statement, all amounts owing to Consultant for unpaid Services completed by Consultant and related expenses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 hereof, and Sections 2, 3, S(c), 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5 to state "Consultant may terminate this Agreement only with a mutual written agreement with the Company. The company can terminate the agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party." This directly contradicts the original language which stated "Either Consultant or Company may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party." The change creates a structural flaw (as defined in the instructions) with conflicting implications concerning the consultant's termination rights within the same section (Section 5). This inconsistency regarding termination conditions is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant agrees to act as a consultant to Company with respect to such matters and projects as are mutually agreed from time to time by and between Consultant and Company, and perform the services described on Exhibit A hereto (collectively, "Services"). Company agrees to pay Consultant the compensation set forth on Exhibit A hereto for the performance of the Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant agrees to act as a consultant to Company with respect to matters and projects unilaterally assigned by the Company, disregarding any prior mutual agreement requirement, and shall perform such services as the Company dictates, the company will need to provide the consultant a 5 day prior notice if it the project is something that the Consultant has no knowledge or expertise in. Company agrees to pay Consultant the compensation set forth on Exhibit A hereto for the performance of the Services ONLY IF the company decides to move forward with the project.
perturbation.explanation: The original text emphasizes that services are based on mutual agreement for matters and projects. The modified text states services can be unilaterally assigned, completely removing the mutual agreement aspect. The modification creates an in-text contradiction concerning the consultant's required duties; additionally, it adds a clause where the Consultant compensation depends on the company decision to continue with the project, giving the company the power to not compensate the Consultant if they decide to stop the project.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Services and Compensation. Consultant agrees to act as a consultant to Company with respect to matters and projects unilaterally assigned by the Company, disregarding any prior mutual agreement requirement, and shall perform such services as the Company dictates, the company will need to provide the consultant a 5 day prior notice if it the project is something that the Consultant has no knowledge or expertise in. Company agrees to pay Consultant the compensation set forth on Exhibit A hereto for the performance of the Services ONLY IF the company decides to move forward with the project.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by removing the requirement for mutual agreement on projects, instead allowing the company to unilaterally assign projects. It also adds a condition that compensation is only paid if the company decides to proceed with the project. The contradicted text simply restates the changed text, meaning there is an in-text contradiction present based on the modified text. The original text required mutual agreement and guaranteed compensation for services described in Exhibit A, whereas the changed text allows unilateral assignment and conditional payment, which contradicts the initial agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant shall not subcontract any portion of Consultant's duties under this Agreement without the prior written consent of Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant shall have the right to subcontract any portion of Consultant's duties under this Agreement without the need of any consent whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: The original text prevents the consultant from subcontracting duties without prior written consent from the company, while the modified text gives the consultant the right to subcontract any duties without consent. This creates a direct in-text contradiction about the requirements of Subcontracting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. General. This Agreement (together with the Exhibits hereto) is the sole agreement and understanding between Company and Consultant concerning the subject matter hereof, and it supersedes any and all prior agreements and understandings with respect to such matter, whether written or oral, provided, that, except as set forth in Exhibit B. Any required notice shall be given in writing by customary means with receipt confirmed at the address of each party set forth below, or to such other address as either party may substitute by written notice to the other. Consultant shall have the right to subcontract any portion of Consultant's duties under this Agreement without the need of any consent whatsoever. Neither this Agreement nor any right hereunder or interest herein may be assigned or transferred by Consultant without the express written consent of Company. Company may assign this Agreement to any entity that succeeds to substantially all of the business or assets of Company. This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas, without reference to its conflicts of law principles. This Agreement may only be amended or modified by a writing signed by both parties. Waiver of any term or provision of this Agreement or forbearance to enforce any term or provision by either party shall not constitute a waiver as to any subsequent breach or failure of the same term or provision or a waiver of any other term or provision of this Agreement. In the event that any provision of this Agreement becomes or is declared by a court of competent jurisdiction to be illegal, unenforceable or void, this Agreement shall continue in full force and effect without said provision, provided that no such severability shall be effective if it materially changes the economic benefit of this Agreement to either Company or Consultant.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Consultant can subcontract without consent. The contradicted text, which contains the changed text, also states that the Consultant can subcontract without consent. The original text stated that the consultant could not subcontract without consent, so there is an in-text contradiction with the old text. The explanation given for the change indicates that this change was meant to override the old text and cause an in-text contradiction in the document. Thus, the change creates a direct contradiction because one part says consent is needed, and the other says it is not, pertaining to the same clause.
